description: Integração com Calendário do Outlook via Microsoft Graph API — criar, atualizar e cancelar eventos automaticamente ao agendar follow-ups de PDI e feedbacks futuros

userStories.title: Add Microsoft token and Outlook event ID fields to database schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to store Microsoft OAuth tokens per user and Outlook event IDs on follow-up/feedback records so the system can manage calendar events via Graph API.
acceptanceCriteria: Add fields to User model: msAccessToken String?, msRefreshToken String?, msTokenExpiresAt DateTime?
Add field to PDIFollowUp model: outlookEventId String?
Add field to Feedback model: outlookEventId String?
Generate and apply migration with prisma migrate dev
Run prisma generate without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All fields are nullable so no data migration is needed. Combine all changes in one migration file.

userStories.title: Configure Microsoft OAuth scopes and persist tokens on SSO login
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the system to request calendar permissions during SSO login and store the resulting tokens so they can be used for Graph API calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Add authorization params to Microsoft Entra ID provider in src/lib/auth.ts: scope includes 'openid profile email Calendars.ReadWrite offline_access'

In the jwt callback of src/lib/auth.ts, when provider is 'microsoft-entra-id' and account object is present, persist account.access_token, account.refresh_token, and account.expires_at to the User record via prisma.user.update
Existing jwt callback logic for setting token.id, token.role, token.evaluationMode is preserved unchanged
Protected file: EXTEND src/lib/auth.ts, do NOT replace it or change its structure
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/lib/auth.ts is a protected file per CLAUDE.md. Only add the scope config to the provider and token persistence logic inside the existing jwt callback. account.expires_at is a Unix timestamp (seconds) — convert to Date for msTokenExpiresAt.

userStories.title: Create Microsoft Graph calendar utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable Graph API client so all calendar operations share the same token refresh and HTTP logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new file src/lib/microsoft-graph.ts

Export type GraphCalendarEvent with fields: subject (string), start (object with dateTime and timeZone), end (object with dateTime and timeZone), body (object with contentType and content), attendees (array of objects with emailAddress and type)

Export async function getUserToken(userId: string): Promise<string | null> — reads User from DB, returns null if no msAccessToken, refreshes via refresh_token if expired (using POST to https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId}/oauth2/v2.0/token), updates DB with new tokens, returns valid access token

Export async function createCalendarEvent(accessToken: string, event: GraphCalendarEvent): Promise<string | null> — POSTs to https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/events, returns event id on success, null on error
Export async function updateCalendarEvent(accessToken: string, eventId: string, event: Partial<GraphCalendarEvent>): Promise<boolean> — PATCHes event, returns true on success
Export async function deleteCalendarEvent(accessToken: string, eventId: string): Promise<boolean> — DELETEs event, returns true on success
All Graph API errors are caught with try/catch and logged via console.error, never propagated
Uses environment variables AZURE_AD_CLIENT_ID, AZURE_AD_CLIENT_SECRET, AZURE_AD_TENANT_ID for token refresh
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use native fetch (no external HTTP library needed). Token refresh endpoint: POST https://login.microsoftonline.com/{AZURE_AD_TENANT_ID}/oauth2/v2.0/token with grant_type=refresh_token, client_id, client_secret, refresh_token, scope='https://graph.microsoft.com/.default'. Import prisma from @/lib/prisma for DB access.

userStories.title: Add time input to PDI follow-up scheduling form and action
userStories.description: As a gestor, I want to choose a start time when scheduling a PDI follow-up so the Outlook event reflects the correct meeting time.
acceptanceCriteria: In FollowUpsSection component in src/components/pdi-tracking.tsx, add a state variable scheduleTime with default '09:00'
Add <input type='time'> with label 'Horário de início' next to the existing date input in the schedule form
Pass scheduleTime as third argument when calling scheduleFollowUp
Update scheduleFollowUp function in src/app/(dashboard)/pdis/actions.ts to accept optional third parameter scheduledTime: string (default '09:00')
The scheduledTime parameter is accepted but not yet used (will be used in US-006)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The FollowUpsSection is in src/components/pdi-tracking.tsx around lines 1074-1340. The schedule form is around lines 1192-1230. There are two places where scheduleFollowUp is called: (1) the main schedule form, (2) the 'schedule next' modal after completing a follow-up. Add time input to both.

userStories.title: Add time input to future feedback scheduling form and action
userStories.description: As a gestor, I want to choose a start time when scheduling a future feedback so the Outlook event reflects the correct meeting time.
acceptanceCriteria: In the future feedback modal in src/components/feedback-table.tsx, add a state variable futureTime with default '09:00'
Add <input type='time'> with label 'Horário de início' next to the existing date input in the modal
Pass futureTime in the data object when calling createFutureFeedback
Update createFutureFeedback function in src/app/(dashboard)/feedbacks/actions.ts to accept optional scheduledTime field in the data parameter (default '09:00')
The scheduledTime parameter is accepted but not yet used (will be used in US-007)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The future feedback modal is in src/components/feedback-table.tsx around lines 465-549. The createFutureFeedback action is in src/app/(dashboard)/feedbacks/actions.ts around lines 553-622.

userStories.title: Create Outlook event when scheduling PDI follow-up
userStories.description: As a gestor, I want a calendar event to be created in mine and the employee's Outlook when I schedule a PDI follow-up so that both parties are reminded automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: In scheduleFollowUp in src/app/(dashboard)/pdis/actions.ts, after creating the PDIFollowUp record, call getUserToken for both the manager (session user) and the employee

For each user with a valid token, call createCalendarEvent with: subject='Acompanhamento PDI — [Employee Name]', body='Acompanhamento de PDI agendado pelo sistema. Acesse: {NEXTAUTH_URL}/pdis/{pdiId}', start=scheduledAt date + scheduledTime (or 09:00 default), end=start + 1 hour, timeZone='America/Sao_Paulo'
Use Promise.allSettled for parallel event creation (never Promise.all)
Save the manager's outlookEventId to the PDIFollowUp record via prisma update
If getUserToken returns null for a user, skip silently (no error)
If createCalendarEvent fails, the follow-up is still created successfully (no rollback)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Need to fetch the employee name and the PDI's employeeId to get tokens for both parties. The manager is the session user. Use process.env.NEXTAUTH_URL for the link in the body.

userStories.title: Create Outlook event when scheduling future feedback
userStories.description: As a gestor, I want a calendar event to be created when I schedule a future feedback so both parties have it in their Outlook.
acceptanceCriteria: In createFutureFeedback in src/app/(dashboard)/feedbacks/actions.ts, after creating the Feedback record, call getUserToken for both the manager and the employee

For each user with a valid token, call createCalendarEvent with: subject='Feedback — [Employee Name]', body='Feedback agendado pelo sistema. Acesse: {NEXTAUTH_URL}/feedbacks/{feedbackId}', start=scheduledAt date + scheduledTime (or 09:00 default), end=start + 1 hour, timeZone='America/Sao_Paulo'
Use Promise.allSettled for parallel event creation
Save the manager's outlookEventId to the Feedback record via prisma update
If getUserToken returns null, skip silently
If createCalendarEvent fails, the feedback is still created successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Similar pattern to US-006. The manager is the session user (managerId). The employee is data.employeeId. Use process.env.NEXTAUTH_URL for the link.

userStories.title: Cancel Outlook event when cancelling PDI follow-up
userStories.description: As a gestor, I want the Outlook event to be deleted when I cancel a PDI follow-up so it disappears from calendars.
acceptanceCriteria: In cancelFollowUp in src/app/(dashboard)/pdis/actions.ts, before or after updating status to 'cancelled', check if the PDIFollowUp record has an outlookEventId
If outlookEventId exists, get the manager's token via getUserToken and call deleteCalendarEvent
Failure to delete the Outlook event does not prevent the follow-up cancellation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Need to fetch the follow-up record first to check for outlookEventId. The manager is the session user.

userStories.title: Cancel or update Outlook event when feedback is cancelled or rescheduled
userStories.description: As a gestor, I want the Outlook event to be removed or updated when a feedback is cancelled or its date changes.
acceptanceCriteria: In cancelScheduledFeedback in src/app/(dashboard)/feedbacks/actions.ts, if the Feedback record has outlookEventId, call deleteCalendarEvent via the manager's token
In rescheduleFeedback in src/app/(dashboard)/feedbacks/actions.ts, if the Feedback record has outlookEventId, call updateCalendarEvent with the new scheduledAt date+time
Failures in Outlook API calls do not prevent the database operations
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: cancelScheduledFeedback and rescheduleFeedback are both in src/app/(dashboard)/feedbacks/actions.ts. Need to import getUserToken, deleteCalendarEvent, updateCalendarEvent from src/lib/microsoft-graph.ts.
